app.name: DevEnv
profile.noVars: No variables yet. Click "Add Variable" to get started.
settings.title: Settings
onboarding.step1.title: Welcome to DevEnv
onboarding.step1.desc: Manage your dev environment config in one place. No more hand-editing .bashrc.
onboarding.step1.d2: Switch dev contexts in one click
onboarding.step1.d3: Proxy, runtime versions, PATH — all in one place
onboarding.step2.title: Create a Profile
onboarding.step2.desc: A Profile is a collection of environment variables. Create different profiles for different work scenarios.
onboarding.step2.d1: Click the + button at the top of the sidebar to create a new Profile
onboarding.step3.title: Manage Variables
onboarding.step3.desc: Add, edit, and delete environment variables in the right panel.
onboarding.step3.d1: Click "Add Variable" to add a new one
onboarding.step3.d2: Enter key and value, e.g. JAVA_HOME=/opt/jdk-17
onboarding.step3.d3: Use the toggle to enable/disable individual variables
onboarding.step4.title: Activate a Profile
onboarding.step4.desc: Select a Profile and click Activate to enable it.
onboarding.step4.d1: Only one Profile can be active at a time
onboarding.step4.d2: Active profiles show a green dot and Active badge
onboarding.step4.d3: Double-click a profile in the sidebar to quickly activate it
onboarding.step5.title: Terminal Integration
onboarding.step5.desc: DevEnv needs to inject a source command into your shell config so new terminals automatically load the active profile.
onboarding.hook.clickToInject: Click the button below to automatically append the hook to your shell config.
onboarding.hook.restart: Open a new terminal to apply changes.
